say.text: TOKYO, PRESENT DAY. Mori Daiki, 42. Chief Financial Officer of Hoshizora Trading. Married, one son, one mortgage, zero enemies. Until Tuesday.
On Tuesday you found the Fourth Ledger — the one that isn't in the system. Two decades of laundered money flowing through your company like a sewer under a shrine.
I did everything right. Twenty years of doing everything right.
On Wednesday your CEO introduced you to a man with no neck and a Kurosawa-gumi pin. The message was simple: the ledger is yours now. The debt is yours now. The FAMILY is yours now.
Fine. I'll smile. I'll bow. I'll carry their filth in my briefcase… and I will document every single yen of it. You bought an accountant, Kurosawa. You have no idea what accountants remember.
The plaza east of your office. Mogi, the debt collector, waits with two thugs and your first "assignment" — beating a payment out of a man who looks like you did on Tuesday.
First day, salaryman! Here's the job: Watanabe owes. You collect. Or we collect YOU. Consider it… onboarding.
(Play along. Play along. Play al—) He has a family, Mogi.
So do you. Oh — was that the wrong thing to say? Your face says it was the wrong thing to say.
You know what, Mogi? Let's review your performance instead.
Mogi wheezes on the pavement between two neat rows of his own teeth.
You're… insane… the King runs collections in Kabukicho… he'll bury you under the club…
Kabukicho, then. (And Mogi — you just became my first exhibit.)
Overhead, the crossing signal has been blinking the same half-second of green for three minutes. Nobody else seems to notice. The metro junction sky looks… torn.
At the metro junction the rain stops mid-air. A crane three blocks away hangs frozen mid-swing. Ten thousand commuters stand perfectly, horribly still.
The sky between the towers TEARS. Purple light. And through the wound you glimpse a burning village, a man with a katana — and a neon desert.
TWO OF THREE. FOUND. THE LOCK TURNS.
The ledger — the numbers are moving — they're the same numbers, they were ALWAYS the same numbers — WHO ARE YOU?
Your reflection in the office glass answers. It draws a sword you have never held, and aims a gun you have never fired.
— DAIKI'S STORY REACHES THE RIFT —
